title: claim-next selection: own lane first, approved lanes only, hold-skip, typed stop (lib + CLI, RED-first)
action: Ship the claim-next selection as lib + CLI (per D2; additive per D4). SWEEP PIN (panel B1 — without this, a dead session's stale claim makes its cell unclaimable forever and produces FALSE 'no approved work' stops): selection RUNS sweepExpiredClaims (claims.mjs — gate-verified: TTL expired AND heartbeat stale, pattern 20260710) at its start, so a swept cell is claimable in the same pass, and the typed NO_APPROVED_WORK stop may only be returned after the sweep ran; the claim-next verb is thereby the production sweep trigger (sweepExpiredClaims currently has ZERO production callers — tests only). Selection, given the acting session: (1) own bound lane's ready cells first (deps capped, that lane's execution gate approved per B13); (2) lane empty (or session unbound with the default pipeline empty) → ready cells of OTHER lanes whose execution gate is approved ONLY — an unapproved lane is never touched (D2's authority boundary); (3) skip any cell whose files intersect another session's active holds (findSessionConflicts in reservations.mjs); (4) cross-lane ordering: backlog rank then lane created_at. ORDERING REALITY (do not assume): rankBacklog in backlog.mjs returns the ID-column order only and its parser never reads the Feature column; docs/backlog.md's table is | ID | Story | CoS | Status | Feature | — BUILD a small exported helper in backlog.mjs that maps feature slug → rank position by parsing the Feature column (rows with Feature '—' and lanes without a row order last, by lane created_at; backlog.mjs imports only fs/path so no circular-import risk). (5) nothing claimable → typed {ok:false, code:'NO_APPROVED_WORK'}. Claiming is the two-store sequence with rollback: claims.mjs claimCellFile FIRST, then cells.mjs claimCell — UNWIND PIN (panel W4): claimCell signals EVERY failure by THROW (never {ok:false}); wrap it in try/catch and releaseClaim on any throw — a return-value check would orphan the claim file on every real failure. A crash between the two steps is cured by the claim TTL + the selection sweep (document in the helper comment). CLI: one verb (cells.claim-next or state.claim-next — pick what reads naturally beside existing groups), one entry + one handler in bee.mjs's HANDLERS map + an exercised runExample row (harness in test_bee_cli.mjs ~:116). Zero-lane/no-session repos: every existing row passes unmodified. RED-first with RED evidence. Vendor byte-identical siblings (cmp chain in verify).

truths: A cell claimed by a dead session (TTL expired + heartbeat stale) is reclaimable by claim-next in one pass — the stale claim is swept and the cell selected; NO_APPROVED_WORK is never returned while such a cell exists
claim-next picks own-lane ready work first, pulls only from execution-approved other lanes, and never selects from an unapproved lane even when its cells are the only ready ones
Cells whose files intersect another session's active holds are skipped; the acting session's own holds do not exclude a cell
A cells.mjs claimCell THROW after the claim file was created releases the claim file (no orphan)
Every existing suite row passes unmodified (zero-lane/no-session byte-parity)

artifacts.substantive: claim-next selection with in-pass stale-claim sweep, lane authority, hold-skip, backlog-rank ordering, throw-safe two-store claim

key_links: selection sweeps via claims.mjs sweepExpiredClaims then consults lane gates via the fsh-3 resolution API and holds via findSessionConflicts; the new backlog helper maps feature→rank from the Feature column; the CLI verb has an exercised runExample row
prohibitions: No weakened or deleted existing assertion

verify: node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/cells.mjs .bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/backlog.mjs .bee/bin/lib/backlog.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/command-registry.mjs .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs .bee/bin/bee.mjs

trace.outcome: Shipped claim-next: cross-session selection (own bound lane first, then execution-approved other lanes only, hold-skip, backlog-rank+created_at cross-lane ordering) that runs sweepExpiredClaims in-pass as its production trigger, plus a throw-safe two-store claim (claimCellFile then cells.mjs claimCell, unwound via releaseClaim on any throw). New backlog.mjs helper featureBacklogRank maps feature slug to rank position from the Feature column. New CLI verb cells.claim-next through the unified registry with an exercised runExample row.
trace.verification_evidence:
{
  "tests_inspected": [
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs (cells.mjs, claims.mjs, reservations.mjs, state.mjs, backlog.mjs coverage)",
    "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs (COMMAND_REGISTRY + dispatcher contract, DA5 bijection, executedNames coverage)"
  ],
  "tests_added_or_changed": [
    "test_lib.mjs: featureBacklogRank maps feature slug -> rank position (Feature column, placeholder rows excluded, missing file -> empty map)",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell sweeps a dead session's stale claim (TTL expired + heartbeat stale) in-pass and selects it in the same call (C10 production sweep trigger)",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell picks the acting session's own bound lane first even when a backlog-favored other approved lane also has a ready cell",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell never selects from an unapproved own lane even when its cell is the only ready one anywhere (typed NO_APPROVED_WORK)",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell falls through to another execution-approved lane when the own lane has no ready cells",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell cross-lane ordering by backlog rank then lane created_at",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell skips a cell held by another session's active reservation; the acting session's own hold never excludes it",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimCellCrossSession releases the claims-store file (no orphan) when cells.mjs's own claimCell throws (uncapped deps forcing a real throw)",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell works with no lanes and no prior session record (D4 zero-lane shape)",
    "test_lib.mjs: claimNextCell returns typed NO_APPROVED_WORK when nothing is claimable anywhere",
    "test_bee_cli.mjs: cells.claim-next registry entry executed via its own exercised runExample row against a fresh fixture cell"
  ],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Before this cell: sweepExpiredClaims (claims.mjs) had zero production callers anywhere in the codebase (grep confirmed only test callers) — a dead session's stale claim could wedge its cell unclaimable forever with no code path ever reclaiming it, and no claim-next verb existed at all (grep for 'claimNextCell'/'claim-next' across skills/bee-hive/templates and docs/history/fresh-session-handoff pre-cell returned nothing but the plan/context prose naming the gap). rankBacklog (backlog.mjs) parsed docs/backlog.md's Status column only and never read the Feature column, so no feature-slug-to-rank lookup existed for cross-lane ordering. First real suite run after writing the new tests + implementation (before vendoring templates/ into .bee/bin/) was captured red: test_lib.mjs reported '290 passed, 1 failed' — the pre-existing 'vendored source: every templates/*.mjs and templates/lib/*.mjs is byte-identical to its .bee/bin sibling' guard failed because cells.mjs/backlog.mjs/command-registry.mjs/bee.mjs had been edited under skills/bee-hive/templates/ but not yet copied to .bee/bin/ — the standing byte-parity guard is deliberately allergic to exactly the one-sided-edit gap this cell would otherwise ship with. After `cp` vendoring all four files, the full verify chain (test_lib.mjs 291/291, test_bee_cli.mjs 121/121, test_onboard_bee.mjs 0 failures/1 pre-existing unrelated skip, all 4 cmp byte-parity checks) went green.",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_cli.mjs && node skills/bee-hive/scripts/test_onboard_bee.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/cells.mjs .bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/backlog.mjs .bee/bin/lib/backlog.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/command-registry.mjs .bee/bin/lib/command-registry.mjs && cmp -s skills/bee-hive/templates/bee.mjs .bee/bin/bee.mjs — exit 0, all green."
}
